name: My Digital Twin
description: An AI-powered chatbot that represents me on my personal website. Visitors can have a natural conversation with it to learn about my background, skills, experience, and projects — as if they were chatting with me directly.
role: Sole developer — designed and built end-to-end
infrastructure: Vercel for both the Python serverless backend and Next.js frontend
highlights: System prompt is dynamically assembled from structured personal data (LinkedIn PDF, JSON facts, plain-text notes) so the twin stays accurate without touching code
In-memory session management supports multi-turn conversation across a visit
FastAPI backend deployed as a serverless Python function on Vercel with full CORS and routing config
Originally built with AWS Lambda, API Gateway, S3, CloudFront, and Terraform; later simplified to a pure Vercel deployment

name: Cognis
description: An AI-powered incident intelligence platform that sits between alerting tools and on-call engineers. When an alert fires, Cognis intercepts it, runs it through a RAG-augmented tool-calling agent that retrieves relevant runbooks and investigates live signals, and delivers a structured incident brief to the engineer — reducing mean time to resolution.
role: Sole developer — designed and built end-to-end
infrastructure: ECS Fargate for the FastAPI backend, S3 for the React frontend, DynamoDB for storage, S3 Vectors for the vector index, SQS and Lambda for async notifications and corpus ingestion, all on AWS
highlights: Multi-stage AI pipeline: alert normalisation (Grafana, PagerDuty, generic) → triage classifier → retrieve-then-rerank RAG (S3 Vectors + Cohere Rerank 3.5) → tool-calling reasoning agent → LLM-as-judge quality evaluation

Reasoning agent uses Claude Sonnet 4.6 with four tools — get_metrics, get_deployment_history, search_incident_history, get_service_dependencies — to actively investigate incidents rather than reason from static context alone
RAG pipeline retrieves top 20 candidates via vector similarity then reranks to top 5 using Cohere Rerank 3.5 before passing context to the agent
LLM-as-judge runs asynchronously after every analysis, scoring groundedness, completeness, and actionability — scores stored internally for observability without being exposed to engineers
Extensible provider pattern using abstract base classes — new notification channels (Slack, Teams) and alert sources (Datadog, OpsGenie) added with one new file and zero changes to existing code
Resolved incidents are automatically re-ingested into the RAG corpus via SQS and Lambda, making the system smarter over time

Full observability stack: structlog JSON logging with X-Ray trace correlation, AWS X-Ray distributed tracing, Langfuse AI pipeline tracing, and custom EMF metrics for pipeline latency, agent tool calls, and judge quality scores

name: Meridian Customer Support Agent
description: An AI-powered customer support chatbot for Meridian Electronics that handles product discovery, order placement, order history lookup, and customer authentication through natural conversation. Built as a production-ready prototype using a FastAPI MCP agent backend and a streaming React chat interface.
role: Sole developer — designed and built end-to-end
infrastructure: ECS EC2 for the FastAPI backend behind an Application Load Balancer, S3 static hosting for the React frontend, AWS Secrets Manager for runtime secrets, all provisioned with Terraform and deployed via GitHub Actions with OIDC
highlights: MCP-powered agent loop — FastAPI backend connects to a remote MCP server over Streamable HTTP, discovers tools at startup, and runs a multi-iteration tool-calling agent loop with SSE streaming to the frontend

Conversational authentication — agent identifies when a protected action is requested (order history, order placement), prompts for email and 4-digit PIN, calls verify_customer_pin, and stores customer identity in session state for the remainder of the conversation

Session-aware agent — customer_id and customer_name are injected into the system prompt on every LLM call so the agent never re-asks for credentials and always uses the correct identity when calling order tools

Order placement flow — agent fetches current product price via get_product, summarises the order with total cost, and waits for explicit customer confirmation before calling create_order, preventing accidental submissions

Production-grade error handling — unknown tool name validation, jsonschema argument validation before MCP calls, MCP unreachable guard returning HTTP 503, and exponential backoff retry on LLM rate limit errors

Multi-conversation history — frontend persists multiple conversation threads in localStorage, each with its own session ID, allowing customers to switch between past conversations with full message history restored

Clean modular architecture — agent loop, tool handling, prompt building, session management, and MCP client each live in isolated modules so any layer can be replaced or extended without touching the others
